metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_2884
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Fig.2: "Association properties of HSP70 with the PSD"
- "As shown in Figure 2C, Hsp70 and the lower band of Hsc70 are not specifically enriched in the PSD fraction. Interestingly, the amount of the upper band (75 kDa) of Hsc70 is dramatically reduced (14.1 ± 2.2%, n = 2, that of synaptosome) in the One-Triton PSD fraction (Fig. 2C, Hsc70, upper arrowhead), indicating that the majority of this protein is removed by 0.5% Triton X-100 and that it may not be an intrinsic member of the PSD."

Fig.5: "Expression of Hsp70 in dissociated hippocampal neurons"
- "The subcellular distribution of Hsc70 immunoreactivity was similar to that of Hsp70 in that most of the puncta were double-labeled with both Hsc70 and PSD-95 antibodies (Fig. 6A)"

Fig.8: "Immunoelectron microscopic localization of Hsp70 in synaptic sites"
- "Immunoreactive structures for Hsc70 were similar to those staining for Hsp70. Asymmetrical synapses (Fig. 8, asterisks), including axospinous ones (Fig. 8B, asterisk), were strongly stained, but symmetrical ones were stained weakly (Fig. 8A, large open arrows). Presynaptic thickenings and synaptic vesicles were stained weakly. Patchy staining was scattered throughout the cytoplasm.". Experimental description: - "The One-Triton PSD fraction (Cho et al., 1992) was prepared from adult rat (Sprague-Dawley) forebrains"
- "Cultures of embryonic day 18 rat hippocampal neurons were grown as described previously"
